Figure 3. Industry’s evolving future.
3.1 Bringing together information and operational
technologies
All of this is enabled by greater integration, visibility and
intelligence within and among the operational technology
(OT), production control systems and information technology
(IT) that manage the company’s critical assets, logistics,
planning and operations. The result is unprecedented agility in
operations and to both supply and demand fluctuations.
As the convergence of IT and OT brings more information
from real-time systems into IT software, the following are
among the four ”must-have” benefits that will enhance
efficiency, responsiveness and profitability across the mining
value chain:
– Smart production
– Intelligent response to critical asset condition
– Demand-driven planning
– Reduced energy consumption and waste
Yet a major challenge for mining companies to reach their
goals is a lack of integration between IT and OT systems. A
growing number of mining companies see the single leading
benefit of IT/OT data integration as o

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper

text:
en IT and OT systems. A
growing number of mining companies see the single leading
benefit of IT/OT data integration as optimizing for cost and
efficiency. This directly addresses the challenges of:
– Managing ever increasing costs
– Controlling energy expenditure and efficiency
– Optimizing or maximizing production
Unfortunately, many companies have little or no data
integration across the value chain and still operate in silos,
with data not being shared with other departments. Many
still rely on spreadsheets combined with human expertise for
crucial decision support.
But, things are changing with several companies now taking
steps to implement IT/OT data integration. These companies
have a consolidated view of production systems and the most
advanced these can dynamically view and adjust operations
across the value chain.
It would appear that a growing number mine operators
understand that IT and OT cannot operate in silos if they are
to continue to deliver good shareholder returns in light of
increasingly difficult extraction realities.
Many are coming to the realization that, to

increasingly difficult extraction realities.
Many are coming to the realization that, to address emerging

ABB has been working for over 10 years to develop and

text:
[PAGE 11]
ABB White Paper | Next Level mining 11
Today a typical mine and process operation has various
areas in which operators run their own machines, often
independently and without much communication or
collaboration between them. There is rarely any integration
between equipment and systems resulting in what is now
frequently being referred to as islands of automation.
The challenge is to optimize the entire value chain, so that
there is a complete and unified view of production, from raw
material to processing, stock pile and delivery.
Integrating IT and OT helps close gaps in the value chain. For
example, with good communication, blasting and crushing in
the mine can be optimized together with the grinding process
in the concentrator as a whole.
Automating every part of a mine brings significant benefits
including:
– Higher productivity, enhanced collaboration
– Holistic commercial view of the whole enterprise
– Increased energy efficiency
– Improved safety

The future of mining lies in bringing equipment, systems and
people together; the technology that can achieve this
is automation.
4.1 Uniting the islands of automation
Taking the mine mentioned above, with its various
independent islands of automation, the idea is to bring
them together so that they talk the same language and are
integrated into one system where all information is available.
While local control rooms may still exist, the trend is towards
centralized operations.
With modern control room designs, people with different
roles can work together in the same environment. Barriers are
torn down, control rooms are consolidated and experts can
collaborate in one place.
Such extended automation closes gaps in the valu

ontrol rooms are consolidated and experts can
collaborate in one place.
Such extended automation closes gaps in the value chain and
creates a safe, reliable and predictable production process
with better visibility from mine to concentrator, smelter and
stockyard.
The result is an optimized operation from mine to port.

The Garpenberg mine is the first to integrate mine hoists,

vehicles. An integrated maintenance system allows for
remote solutions for the control of the entire mine. In
particular, Boliden uses mobile AF devices to supervise

“It’s not a coincidence that ABB became one of the main
suppliers”, says Lars Brännström, Strategic Account
Manager for Boliden. “Together with the customer, we
discussed and developed the best solutions for the mine.”
Lars emphasizes how important it is to work closely with
the customer to understand their needs, to listen carefully
and to be flexible and make necessary changes during
the project.

5.2 Extended operator workplace (EOW)
The EOW is a modern cockpit for mining operations. System
800xA ensures that information is available centrally to all
users and locally, using mobile devices such as tablets or
smartphones.
An EOW avoids the cluttered and scattered overview that
operators currently endure. This environment can result in
poor collaboration in which operators never have a true
overview of the process and are more likely to make mistakes.
In fact the global process industry loses $20 billion, or
five percent of annual production, due to poor quality and
unscheduled downtime. Consultants from the ARC Group
estimate that almost 80 percent of these losses are preventable
and 42 percent are primarily the result of operator error.
Integrating the data and presenting it through an EOW will
enhance efficiency, responsiveness and profitability across
the mining value chain. Integration on this scale will become
the norm for all leading mining companies over the next few
years — indeed, some are already well down this path.

The software solutions include planning, logistics, sales
and marketing, asset management and business analytics

of leading-edge hardware, operational and information
technologies from one global vendor has opened new
horizons for mining companies looking to embark on
productivity transformations.

ABB’s position in mining is to supply the complete data

As a technology company with a long history of providing

brings a deep understanding of how challenges can be

6.0 Areas of automation
6.1 Process and power control system core
With the vast power consumption of a modern mine, it makes
sense to integrate the data from the electrical side with the
rest of the operation. In this way, the power consumers —
electrical machines, hoists, conveyer belts, ventilation, lighting
and emergency systems — can work in harmony with the
power supply infrastructure.
6.1.1 Mine electrification and its integration with
process control
By integrating the process control and power control within
the same system, capital expenditure (capex) is reduced, as is
the need for training, IT infrastructure and cybersecurity.
Integrating this data within System 800xA with that from other
parts of the enterprise achieves tangible savings. Maintenance
procedures can then be tuned to take into account the
condition of the machine to be serviced along with the wider
implications of taking equipment off line. Maintenance can
be scheduled when other related equipment is nearing a
service interval – or perhaps when market data indi

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B14_ABB_Next_Level_Mining_White_Paper.pdf
text:
intenance can
be scheduled when other related equipment is nearing a
service interval – or perhaps when market data indicates an
upcoming dip in demand.
Significant energy savings can be achieved by combining
market demand and production data - allowing machine
activity to be optimally scheduled. There is no point in
having maximum production rates (and thus highest energy
consumption) if some bottleneck elsewhere in the enterprise
is throttling production requirements.
Integrating process and power automation into one single
automation system like the System 800xA reduces cost,
enables effective power management and provides the mine
operator with the starting point to make the technology step
change now required by the industry.

6.1.2 Wireless communications
Integration of communications improves production efficiency.
Once a wireless communication infrastructure is established
and the mobile and fixed equipment fleet is integrated into
this network, entirely new worlds of data exchange and
optimization possibilities open up.
For example, ABB and Atlas Copco Underground Rock
Excavation, Sweden, have developed an innovative mobile
integration involving System 800xA and Atlas Copco’s mining
machines. The solution is currently installed in a mine in
Kvarntorp, Sweden, and features the integration of real-time
data from mobile machines, fans, faces and draw points, on-
site crushers, conveyor and hoisting systems.
6.1.3 Resource location and data integration
Results reported by mobile equipment - such as online
production reports or machine telemetric information - can be
retrieved and analyzed in the context of location and status,
including local environmental data.

6.0 Areas of automation
6.2 Minerals control and optimization applications
Information integration will dramatically improve production
efficiency and productivity. This is particularly
true when applications are created that exploit that
information in the right context. The whole then becomes
more than the sum of the parts. In this section a few
examples are discussed.
6.2.1 Real-time asset monitoring meets process control
By using real-time condition data, in this case the temperature
sensors on a conveyer drive, the actual condition of the asset
can be detected, allowing proactive actions to be initiated
automatically, greatly reducing the risk of potential failure which
would cause a production loss.
There is a trend for putting planning and reporting and
accounting systems more online.
6.2.2 Smart ventilation and mine dewatering systems
In some mines, ventilation accounts for almost 50 percent of
energy consumption. Mine vehicles emit diesel exhaust gases
that have to be ventilated away.
ABB’s automation system knows the whereabo

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper

text:
ion. Mine vehicles emit diesel exhaust gases
that have to be ventilated away.
ABB’s automation system knows the whereabouts of each
vehicle. And, all vehicle data (including emission levels) is
communicated to the system. The ventilation can therefore be
continuously optimized for actual need and the air flow can be
adjusted at any time.
To manage ventilation on demand, a new method for mine-
wide coordinated control of fans and air regulators optimizes
energy use by automatically feeding the mine with the
required air. The solution relies on feedback from gas, flow or
temperature sensors.
Multi-variable models describe how changes in the speed
of fans affect both the airflow and the pressure across fans.
The parameters in the models are obtained empirically from
operational data, which makes the model easily adaptable for
new conditions.
Air is provided where it is needed in the mine by adjusting
the fan speed based on the air demand in various airways,
properties of the fans and their motors and as well as the
opening angles of the air regulators that are used to control
the air fl

6.2.3 Ore tracking
In ore-processing plants, the largest source of uncertainty
relates to the properties of the ore; plants need to be able
to react quickly to any changes. Much better results can
be obtained when the ore properties are quantified ahead
of time.
Technology can track material movements and properties all
the way from the mine to the processing plant. The software
used to deliver this functionality is able to model the ore
movements along the material handling system, including
conveyo

6.2.4 Grinding circuit
A milling circuit is a complex, multi-variable interacting
system. Dynamically changing ore conditions and wear
parameters pose particular problems for grinding controllers.
By integrating data, the automation system optimizes grinding
controls, avoiding the need for constant manual tuning.
Again, data integration is key. The grinding process can not
only be optimized within itself, but because it has access
to relevant data from the rest of the mining operation, it can
obtain far more leverage. Feed-forward data on ore quality
and quantity, rock face production rates or market demand
rates can be used to optimize the grinding stage.
6.2.5 Flotation circuit
Automatic control can lead to significant flotation performance
improvement. The controller needs to stabilize the process
and to maximize the concentrate production, while
guaranteeing a minimum concentrate quality. This pushes
the process to an upper limit operating point. To achieve this,
optimal set points are automatically chosen by the controller.
Online

optimal set points are automatically chosen by the controller.
Online analyzers and blending algorithms reconcile actual feed
with quality specifications for better predictability.
The mine operator can now increase plant recovery and
optimize product mix by leveraging ore monitoring and
advanced process control (APC) across processes and
business systems. APC manages blending, grinding and
flotation in real-time. Geological models and real-time
material tracking provide a short-term feed characteristic

quality in real-time.

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper

6.2.6 Mine operation scheduling
Another typical mining scenario which could be optimized
through automation is scheduling. Let us assume that a
drilling cycle has missed a blast round. In this case the
disturbance is putting the production at risk. With automation
the local operator has a scheduling system in his workplace
and gets notified immediately. The critical area is clearly
marked and the cause of the problem is identified.
Operators and production management can discuss among
themselves and run what-if analysis to simulate different
solutions. Since all staff see the same information, they can
jointly decide what actions to take.
Everything is done in real-time; no need to wait until the end
of the shift.
With access to data from other parts of the operation, mining
methods can move toward a large-scale, continuous mine
operation that can be run as a normal factory.
The extended automation infrastructure created brings
visibility and transparency to operations. The modern operator
can act as a dispatcher or “flight controller”

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B14_ABB_Next_Level_Mining_White_Paper.pdf
text:
ted brings
visibility and transparency to operations. The modern operator
can act as a dispatcher or “flight controller”. This operator
knows the daily and weekly plan, which resources are in place
and has decision-support tools to optimize the usage of these
resources to fulfill the production plan more effectively. This
system allows the operator to detect anomalies faster and re-
assign resources in order to minimize any impact.
Effectively, ABB is enabling manufacturing processes in the
mining environment. This brings improved safety, higher
production and better productivity.
Figure 11. Integration of key information facilitates effective decision-making.

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper

6.0 Areas of automation
Figure 12. 800xA AssetVista TM dashboard.
6.3 Collaborative production management: integrated-
mine-to-port value chain operations
Automation is not new to mining, but the automation
employed in mines is generally more basic than in other
industries. It is often limited to simple control of motors,
equipment or certain parts of processes. Further, mines tend
to have a large number of independent pieces of equipment
and systems from different suppliers. Each of these “islands
of automation” can have its own data, data format and
interfaces, and operators and control room staff must
scrutinize a multitude of conceptually disconnected screens in
order to coordinate different parts of the process.
6.3.1 Predictive asset monitoring
A modern asset optimization system can help mines to go
from reactive to predictive maintenance strategies, avoiding
unnecessary maintenance and reducing operating costs.
Extended automation solutions such as System 800xA can
integrate modern maintenance systems from suppliers such
as IBM,

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B14_ABB_Next_Level_Mining_White_Paper.pdf
text:
Extended automation solutions such as System 800xA can
integrate modern maintenance systems from suppliers such
as IBM, SAP and Ellipse from Ventyx.
A good example of this is the integration of IBM Maximo at
Boliden’s Aitik mine in northern Sweden. With System 800xA,
the operator is alerted by a predictive maintenance alarm if
equipment needs repairing. They can access the maintenance
system, see all upcoming work orders and easily add new
orders. The field engineers can view the same information
on their mobile devices and together they can decide on the
optimal maintenance plan.
Continuous and consistent asset monitoring brings
maintenance management to the operator environment and
provides a single-window interface for all operations related
to asset management. This allows plant personnel to collect,
compare and monitor asset data and thus accurately assess
equipment conditions in real-time.
System 800xA’s asset monitoring functions for mining
combine automation architecture with advanced information
technologies, including integrated fieldbus. Called
AssetVista
TM, the function

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B14_ABB_Next_Level_Mining_White_Paper.pdf
text:
n architecture with advanced information
technologies, including integrated fieldbus. Called
AssetVista
TM, the function monitors all plant assets in real-
time. This includes field devices, control systems and
automation elements, as well as major assets such as heaters
and generators.
The AssetVista
TM architecture and functionality promotes the
creation of complex asset-specific monitors that not only
makes use of the raw device data itself but also integrates it
with process and environmental information to put the data
into context and deliver more precise condition statements.
This concept can be extended to complete production areas
such as hoists, conveyors, mills and grinding and flotation
circuits, thus creating a new level of insight into plant
performance.
System 800xA AssetVista
TM can thus help end users sustain
asset management improvements and improve return on
investment. It is a single point of contact to find the asset
management solution that best fits user needs.

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B14_ABB_Next_Level_Mining_White_Paper.pdf
text:
[PAGE 23]
ABB White Paper | Next Level mining 23
6.3.2 Stockyard management system
Bulk material handling facilities transport material from one of
several locations, such as a mine, to a destination. Often, bulk
material is temporarily stored in stockpiles or stock yards.
Stockpiles are used in many different areas, such as mines,
ports, refineries, power plants or manufacturing facilities.
To increase production efficiency, optimize process output
and reach an accurate and transparent input/output balance,
a high degree of operator support and automation is
necessary. This can be provided by a stockyard management
system (SMS). An SMS is an integrated part of the overall
stockyard management and control system and it includes
instrumentation, electrification and scope for automation of
operator-controlled stockyard machines.
A SMS organizes the stockyard in real-time based on job
definitions generated by production management. The
SMS merges data on incoming material quantities with
additional information such as quality data, ownership of
material and localization and visualizatio

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B14_ABB_Next_Level_Mining_White_Paper.pdf
text:
ial quantities with
additional information such as quality data, ownership of
material and localization and visualization aspects to provide
a comprehensive picture. An “anti-collision system” at the
control level provides for safe operations.
6.3.3 Remote operation centers: visibility and
collaboration
The mining industry is gradually moving towards mine control
through remote operations centers that coordinate multiple
areas within an operation and even across multiple sites.
Local control rooms may still exist in the future, but the trend
is overwhelmingly towards centralized operations — often in
company headquarters. With modern control-room designs
and extended automation, people with different roles can
work together in the same environment.
Control-room consolidation means that barriers are torn down
and experts can collaborate in one place.
Figure 13. Stockpile management system.

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB

text:
[PAGE 24]
24 Next Level mining | ABB White Paper
As all information from local mine sites is available, this
facilitates collaboration in production planning, resource
planning, specialist support, inventories and spare parts,
allowing resources to be optimized across multiple sites.
Data from all parts of the operation can now flow together
to allow precise and optimized management of mining -
from rock face to end customer - across multiple sites.
This collaboration between systems, equipment and people
enables information to be shared without barriers and
empowers operators to perform optimal control actions and
to take sound business decisions.
6.3.4 Real-time value chain optimization
The mining value chain extends from the extraction of
raw materials through the transport of product to the end
customer. To achieve production and productivity targets,
mining companies need to achieve high operational
performance and efficiency across supply chain processes.
Among the principal causes of supply chain bottlenecks are
difficulties in inventory planning, output planning and demand
fo

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B14_ABB_Next_Level_Mining_White_Paper.pdf
text:
g the principal causes of supply chain bottlenecks are
difficulties in inventory planning, output planning and demand
forecasting. These challenges in marrying supply to demand
often result in inefficient production flow, large stockpiles and
the inability to provide customers with the product grades
they desire.
Better integration and automation across processing
plant operations, mine planning and asset maintenance/
management offers visibility into all production variables
including delivery contracts, current inventory, mine plans,
equipment availability and transport schedules and will
guarantee the right product is available at the right time. It
will also make sure that customer orders are accepted only
when the supply chain can deliver, improving negotiating
power and risk management. Real-time material tracking and
geological modelling can provide short-term input to rock
face operations. In addition, this unified view ensures that
equipment maintenance can be planned to suit increases or
decreases in demand.
To further anticipate demand, information can be
supplemented by m

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B14_ABB_Next_Level_Mining_White_Paper.pdf
text:
be planned to suit increases or
decreases in demand.
To further anticipate demand, information can be
supplemented by market-based demand information such
as stock levels, customer demand trends as well as fuel and
commodity pricing. In a coal mining value chain, for instance,
visibility into real-time conditions can be provided by online
coal quality analysis sensors.
When these indicate that the actual blend deviates from
the target, a control sequence could automatically be
triggered that interrogates the inventory data. Based on
stockpile information, reclaiming can then be automatically
adjusted so the customer’s specification is met. The result
is production within specifications, with no penalties and
guaranteed customer satisfaction. This holistic view enables
the full implications of short-term, medium-term or operation-
recovery decisions to be understood, improving the overall
performance of the entire operation.
6.3.5 Big-data-based enterprise optimization
The underground environment takes its toll: the utilization
rate of mobile mining machines can be as low as 20 to 25

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper

ion
The underground environment takes its toll: the utilization
rate of mobile mining machines can be as low as 20 to 25
percent, that of open faces as low as 20 to 30 percent and
some machines can have a mean time before failure as short
as eight hours.
Failure of a critical production asset can have a catastrophic
impact on production targets and profits. The loss resulting
from a main conveyer failure, for example, can run into
hundreds of thousands of dollars per hour.
6.0 Areas of automation

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB

text:

ABB White Paper | Next Level mining 25
A modern asset optimization system can help mines to go
from reactive to predictive maintenance strategies, avoiding
unnecessary maintenance and reducing operating costs.
In future, online access to experts 24/7, online integration
of relevant information and online integration of tools and
inspection devices will be taken for granted. System 800xA
can integrate modern maintenance systems from relevant
suppliers. Real-time data on asset conditions can then be
used to streamline maintenance effectiveness and enable
condition-based monitoring.
In the case of a conveyer system, say, if an asset monitor
were to detect an abnormal condition, an alarm would be
generated and the control system could slow the drive to
reduce failure risk. Once integrated, these systems would
connect directly into the IT systems and automatically raise
a work order with the site maintenance crew. When the crew
completes the work, this could be instantly reported, allowing
the control system to return systems to normal in the shortest
possible time.
In future, t

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper
publisher: ABB
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B14_ABB_Next_Level_Mining_White_Paper.pdf
text:
instantly reported, allowing
the control system to return systems to normal in the shortest
possible time.
In future, the equipment across a company’s mines will be
completely standardized. This not only makes life easier for
operations and maintenance staff, but also it makes a lot of
sense now that each piece of equipment can be included in
the big picture back at the remote operations center.
6.3.6 Multi-mines
Mining companies have become multinational giants with
mines scattered all around the world.
With System 800xA all information from local mines can also
be available and consolidated into corporate headquarters.
This facilitates collaboration across mines. Production
planning, resource planning, specialist support, inventories,
spare parts, etc can be optimized.
By harnessing the full potential of ABB’s extended
automation, mining companies can raise their productivity and
safety, and achieve a more efficient and profitable
mining operation.

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper

text:

26 Next Level mining | ABB White Paper
Conclusion
ABB is committed to the mining industry and has a research
program that aims to extend automation and integration
to even more complex mining scenarios. For instance,
research is underway on the next generation of networking
technologies to enable tracking and communication
of production equipment in open pit and underground
operations. New sensor technology is being developed to
detect mass flow and quality variations more quickly and
accurately across a range of commodities. Meanwhile,
advanced process control models are being explored that will
improve energy efficiency while new asset health monitoring
technology is set to detect more proactively actual and
predicted capacity loss.
A holistic approach to the entire mining operation will feature
smart devices and equipment — enabled for autonomous
configuration, efficient operation and self-diagnostics — as
well as software that delivers total, real-time transparency for
the operators. This will provide visibility of resources across
the mine; intelligent production based

title: ABB Next Level Mining White Paper

text:
transparency for
the operators. This will provide visibility of resources across
the mine; intelligent production based on near real-time
demand, market conditions and available ore types; and an
optimal response to critical asset conditions.
By harnessing the full potential of extended automation
and by bringing people, equipment and systems together
in a fully integrated environment, ABB firmly believes that
mining companies can vastly improve productivity, workforce
satisfaction and safety. The automation journey for the mining
industry has just begun.

Mining industry

Mining supplies key elements for modern technology. Keep up with demand and meet safety and sustainability goals with Siemens. We transform mining sites into digital enterprises, optimizing resources and reducing environmental impact.

Contact us

Overcome industry challenges with digitalization

Modern mining requires enhanced worker safety, improved sustainability and greater operational efficiency and productivity. Companies must adopt digital solutions to meet rising demand and solve these complex challenges.

Enhance safety

Enhance worker safety with advancements in IoT, AI, data analytics and autonomous equipment. Run simulations on a Digital Twin of your mining operations to reinforce safety measures. Integrate analytics to monitor operations.

Ensure sustainability

Transform mining operations into a sustainable digital enterprise. Siemens digital solutions optimize resources and reduce impact, raising mining industry standards for energy efficiency, decarbonization and circularity.

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B18A_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Operations_Excellence_direct_PDF.pdf
text:
[PAGE 1]
Accelerate Mining Operations
Excellence through Integrated
Simulation, Training, & Optimization
by Livia K. Wiley, Product Marketing Manager, SimSci by Schneider Electric
Executive summary
The Metals, Minerals & Mining industry has a resource
problem — operators that are untrained and subject to
a high rate of employee attrition; and plants that operate
at less than optimized conditions. To reverse the trend,
the industry must promote and accelerate mining
operations excellence. It must be done quickly, with
little room for uncertainty.
A single integrated set of simulation, training, and
optimization technologies can enable the industry to
quickly and effectively train operators while running
tightly controlled operations safely, profitably, and in
compliance with environmental regulations. When
coupled together, the realized value of accelerating
mining operations excellence will produce maximized
returns by improving productivity and efficiencies.

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B18A_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Operations_Excellence_direct_PDF.pdf
text:
[PAGE 2]
Schneider Electric White Paper Page 2
Accelerate Mining Operations Excellence Through Integrated Simulation, Training, & Optimization
Introduction
Current Mining
Industry Trends
According to PricewaterhouseCoopers, the world’s top 40 mining companies saw market value
drop 23% and profits by 72% in 2013. The cause? Commodity prices plummeted and political
challenges hindered global operations. But the report’s author notes that the industry isn’t
standing still during these tough times; instead they are “adjusting to tough times in the short-term
with strategies in place to regain confidence.”
Many initiatives and offerings that are available, focus directly on production, energy, and yield
management, with most involving large expenditures that may not drive results for year or more.
Industrial executives are continually searching for new approaches to operations management
that are more aligned with the demands of today’s business environment.
The commodities Metals, Minerals & Mining business remains volatile. The cyclical nature of ore
prices make the market subject to wi

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B18A_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Operations_Excellence_direct_PDF.pdf
text:
ies Metals, Minerals & Mining business remains volatile. The cyclical nature of ore
prices make the market subject to wide swings. The high cost of doing business tops the mining
industry trends and predicting future demand is difficult. Cost controls, return on capital, and
commodity prices are the industry’s biggest concerns.
Trends seem to indicate that companies must maximize returns from existing operations by
improving productivity and efficiencies, not by increasing production volumes. Or said differently,
look at current operating conditions not at expansion. For example, poor project scoping,
integration and performance can leave up to 15% of cost savings on the table. The short supply of
skilled labor only amplifies the problem.
To survive, companies must:
• Mitigate risk when investing in and managing capital projects
• Improve performance, efficiency, and operational effectiveness
• Advance sustainability issues
• Promote talent availability and knowledge transfer
…without ignoring compliance with regulatory requirements.
Over the next 15 years, environmental sustainabili

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B18A_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Operations_Excellence_direct_PDF.pdf
text:
e transfer
…without ignoring compliance with regulatory requirements.
Over the next 15 years, environmental sustainability, rebased globalization, and resource security
will shape and drive the direction of the industry. Companies will have to prove they can deliver in
good times and bad, and remain a good industry to invest and operate in, well into the future.
Addressing Key Challenges
The Metals, Minerals & Mining industry is plagued with untrained operators that tend to leave
after a short time and operations that run at less than optimized conditions. To succeed in today’s
volatile environment, companies must make both financial and time investments in:
• Well-trained Operators: As operators and personnel switch jobs and migrate between
various operations, technology holds the key to engaging the next generation of operators.
While it takes an operational controller roughly 1 year to be efficiently trained, the industry
starts to see the operators switch jobs after 2 years on average. This is where today’s
technology enabling a reduced training time becomes important by accelera

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B18A_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Operations_Excellence_direct_PDF.pdf
text:
fter 2 years on average. This is where today’s
technology enabling a reduced training time becomes important by accelerating careers
and keeping the next generation of workers more engaged and invested with updated
training tools that speed training time. Among the best indicators of the efficiency and
success of any organization is the level and depth of its personnel training. Well trained
employees perform better and, in turn, create a better performing business and an
improved bottom line.

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper
publisher: Schneider Electric

text:
[PAGE 3]
Schneider Electric White Paper Page 3
Accelerate Mining Operations Excellence Through Integrated Simulation, Training, & Optimization
• Optimized Operations: Extracting minerals from ore is an extremely energy-intensive
operation. With the soaring costs and growing demand of construction, along with
globalized competi¬tion, mineral processing complexes are required to achieve very
high throughput rates — all while continually looking for ways to maintain or increase
production and reduce energy consumption. Energy has become one of the highest
variable costs, and in most facilities, the cost changes significantly in real time. Throughput
has emerged as one of the top challenges — specifically, how to maximize production
while balancing utilization and availability.
It’s all about the economics. Taking immediate action in these two areas simultaneously can
turn an unfavorable play into a promising investment. It can drive the market to reexamine the
performance of a company and the entire industry.
Given the current landscape and the unpredictable future of the Metals, Minera

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper
publisher: Schneider Electric

nce of a company and the entire industry.
Given the current landscape and the unpredictable future of the Metals, Minerals & Mining
industry, there has to be a better way to operate. Figure 1 shows the three views of mining
execution — where (i) the Business is interested in how to make money given the volatile nature
of the industry, (ii) Production is concerned with throughput, uptime and waste, while (iii) Process
metrics focus on plant control, asset utilization, and reliability. These three views must be in sync
or one in the same. A single fully-integrated model provides a common interface for planning,
managing, and optimizing operations. An end-to-end solution is the only way to reduce costs,
maximize profit, and minimize risk.
Industry leaders know collaboration between mine, concentrator, smelter and port can help
effectively operate and improve productivity and efficiency. Increasing the profitability of minerals
processing plant operations is achieved by providing measurable performance improvements in
the mills, pyro processes, hydrometallurgy, bulk delivery and port, th

Operations Excellence
KPIs
As Figure 2 shows, Business Performance Measures are defined as the actions that can be
implemented within an operation to maximize profit.
The success of each area — in Asset Management and Operations Management — is tied to
specific Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) with the purpose of operating and optimizing a safe and
profitable facility.
• The left side of Figure 2 shows the progression of Asset Management from reactive, through
preventive and all the way to predictive maintenance. These KPIs look to address the
industry challenge of raising up well-trained and dedicated operators.
• The right side of Figure 2 shows the Operational progression from basic control, to
advanced control, all the way to process optimization. These KPIs are tied to the challenge
of running tightly-controlled optimized operations.
With each step, the performance and profitability of the operation improves and the supporting
t

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B18A_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Operations_Excellence_direct_PDF.pdf
text:
d optimized operations.
With each step, the performance and profitability of the operation improves and the supporting
teams are able to make better, collaborative, and timelier decisions. The convergence of these two
perspectives provides the maximum combined performance of a facility, as measured through real-
time economical and operational metrics.
Translating those steps into executable actions, Figure 3 on the next page shows the mapping
of the individual functions or programs an operator would implement to meet expected KPIs in an
existing operation. On the Asset Management side, the Process group (a.k.a. Maintenance) would
begin with a program of System Checkout Testing, then develop and implement Operator Training,
and finally ensure Safe (and predictable) Operations of the plant. On the Operations Management
side, Production would first consider some Basic Control, then implement Advanced Control
strategies, and finally, perform Real-time Optimization of its operations.

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper

text:
[PAGE 5]
Schneider Electric White Paper Page 5
Accelerate Mining Operations Excellence Through Integrated Simulation, Training, & Optimization
Programs, functions, and metrics, however, are only half the consideration. The only way to
accelerate operations excellence is to use a single set of integrated software tools across the entire
supply chain — from mills to pyro processes to hydrometallurgy to bulk delivery to port — starting
with the asset modeling through operations, and into optimization.
The first step in the solution is to model the asset. If you can’t model your process, you don’t
understand it. If you don’t understand your process, you can’t improve it. And if you can’t
improve your process, you won’t stay competitive. Process simulators are extremely effective
in modeling any mining process — from minerals processing to metals/logistics processing to
end-users / distributors.
Creating dynamic models of the processes allow for design validation, safety and control systems
testing, and optimization of capital expenditure by avoiding costly over-design.
One of the key tas

while maintaining high economic efficiency. In a typical operation, personnel must deal with a
high number of operating limits and operating parameters, which make it impossible to effectively
monitor them and take timely actions. Critical operating situations can be missed which can lead to
serious safety accidents and loss of productivity.

text:
[PAGE 6]
Schneider Electric White Paper Page 6
Accelerate Mining Operations Excellence Through Integrated Simulation, Training, & Optimization
System Checkout Testing
The pre-testing of control logic in a dynamic simulation environment can reduce commissioning
time by several days, and sometimes even weeks. Virtual stimulation software can augment the
dynamic model to design, test, and perform system checkout prior to start-up. Because all functions
are executed within a dynamic environment, it has the capability to bulk configure tie-back models
to aid in training.
Operator Training
Depending on whether the mining operation contains an acid plant for processing the ore, the
complexity of a mining operation varies. Regardless of the complexity, it is important to fully
train operators to safely and efficiently operate the process. In recent years, as plants and their
operating controls and procedures get more and more complex, a fundamental shift in the
workforce has made it necessary for process industry companies to focus on attracting and
retaining the kind of workplace operators

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B18A_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Operations_Excellence_direct_PDF.pdf
text:
s made it necessary for process industry companies to focus on attracting and
retaining the kind of workplace operators they require. Workers in the mining industries last in their
role for a very short time before moving on to something new. This issue has forced the industry
to focus on new, time-efficient training methods, such as Operator Training Simulators (OTS).
Reducing the time it takes to properly train an operator is of utmost importance
Safe Operations
Operational integrity has a significant impact on a facility’s bottom line. The associated costs of lost
production and Health, Safety and Environmental (HSE) incidents can easily justify any expenditure
that improves safety, reliability and performance. A solution in this space must produce measurable
improvements and benefits, including increased facility utilization, improved equipment reliability,
and less downtime. The objective is to reduce maintenance costs while improving safety and
environmental compliance, thus creating a safer, more reliable operation of facility assets.
A brilliant strategy may get things starte

title: Schneider Electric Mining Operations Excellence White Paper
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B18A_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Operations_Excellence_direct_PDF.pdf
text:
ompliance, thus creating a safer, more reliable operation of facility assets.
A brilliant strategy may get things started but without flawless execution, sustainable competitive
advantage isn’t possible. Unfortunately, most companies struggle with implementation of their
strategies. To navigate the business through uncertain conditions and steer it towards future growth,
Operations Management must focus on synchronizing that strategy and execution in real time.
Basic Control
Basic controls are designed and built with the process itself, to facilitate basic operation, control
and automation requirements. The virtual stimulation of DCS or PLC systems, which is built on
algorithms and communication infrastructure of industry control processors, can provide an ideal
environment for designing superior real-time control software, providing high-quality operator
training, and accurately analyzing and troubleshooting control system response and performance.
This saves time and resources.

text:
[PAGE 7]
Schneider Electric White Paper Page 7
Accelerate Mining Operations Excellence Through Integrated Simulation, Training, & Optimization
Advanced Control
In today’s environment, capital budgets and overhead are constantly being cut. Companies are
faced with volatile market swings, soaring energy costs, and an uncertain future. To meet these
challenges, operators are forced to optimize operations and make performance improvements that
will positively affect their bottom line. Greater visibility into legacy processes is needed, even where
basic controls exist, as they are usually insufficient. Advanced Process Control software allows
the operation to function much closer to the operating limits by reducing variations in the process.
This model-based predictive control can often reduce variability by a standard of deviation of two or
greater, thus improving profitability.
Real-time Optimization
Real-time optimization begins with the measurement of throughput, energy, and metallurgical
accounting in real time. Key performance indicators at each consumption, production, transfer,
im

accounting in real time. Key performance indicators at each consumption, production, transfer,
import, or export point in the mill can be quantified and reported. This intelligence can empower
operators to predict impending events which can deliver 3-6% average reduction in energy
consumption and critical production improvements. This same data can be used to reduce
unplanned outages to less than 1 per year and increase utilization by more than 2%. Proactive
guidance allows users to make informed decisions for conflicts, both in the short-term and long-term.

To achieve true operations excellence, a single set of integrated software tools must be used to
execute these functions or programs throughout the lifecycle of an asset. The solution must deliver
a common interface for fulfilling what’s important to all three views of mining execution (Figure 1);
a single model for asset design, simulation, and optimization. A common base of tightly integrated
products ensures a moderate learning curve, transferable skills, and optimized workflow.
A rigorous dynamic simulator can be used during the design stage of a project to execute process
design, control strategy validation, and risk analysis studies. The investment made in these
dynamic simulation models is preserved throughout the later stages of the asset’s lifecycle. For
example, the model can be turned over to Maintenance for meeting safety and training KPIs and/or
to Production for achieving control and optimization metrics.

Production uses the validated dynamic model to advance control system design and

advanced process control are used to determine the optimal operating parameters for a process
to deliver targeted operational results within the context of changing economic and plant needs
and environmental constraints. This comb

Mining, Metals & Minerals

Mining White Paper Series: Sustainability & Optimized Power Systems

Mining White Paper Series: Sustainability & Optimized Power Systems

Published Date

July 1, 2021

Primer: Recommended Power System Design for Mid-Size Iron Ore Mines

title: Schneider Electric Mining Power System White Paper Series
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B19_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Power_System_White_Pap.html
text:
Traditionally, mining power systems have been designed by engineering companies (EPCs) and/or mining end-users applying their industrial process knowledge and their experience from previous projects. This results in robust installations that are often not optimized for cost and footprint because the electrical design process focuses on individual packages rather than considering the whole system. This guide will provide a concrete example of optimization that could be achieved when the end-user, EPC, and manufacturer work together at an early stage of the project.

Download

#1: Network Architecture for Optimized Mining Power System

The choice of power system network architecture and MV distribution voltage have a major impact in the total cost of mining power systems and the availability of electrical power supply. To optimize the power system total cost and its impact on availability, different options need to be evaluated. This paper gives recommendations for electrical network architecture optimization, illustrated by a case study of a mid-size iron ore mine.

Download

title: Schneider Electric Mining Power System White Paper Series
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B19_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Power_System_White_Pap.html
text:
#2: Power transformers for optimized mining power systems

The choice of HV/MV transformer short circuit impedance and MV distribution voltage have a major impact on the total cost of mining power systems. Optimized values depend on constraints imposed by starting large MV motors that can often generate unacceptable voltage drops and harmonic distortion. This white paper provides guidance on how to select HV/MV transformer specification, MV voltage level, and MV motor starting method to minimize the power system TCO.

Download

#3: MV switchgear for mining power system applications - IEC vs ANSI

MV switchgear is a key component of mining power systems as any failure results in loss of production. This paper describes the IEC and ANSI/NEMA standards, MV switchgear types, and technologies used in typical designs for each of the standards. It analyses the advantages and disadvantages of each MV switchgear type for the main applications found in mining power systems. Download to learn practical advice and recommendations to EPCs and mining end users involved in power system design.

title: Schneider Electric Mining Power System White Paper Series
publisher: Schneider Electric
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\raw_docs\B19_Schneider_Electric_Mining_Power_System_White_Pap.html
text:
Download

#4: Recommended Power Generation and Electrical Distribution for High Altitude Lithium Mine

The choice of the power generation and electrical network architecture has a major impact on the total cost of mining power systems and the availability of an electrical power supply. To optimize the power system’s total cost and its impact on availability, different options taking into consideration the effect of altitude, need to be evaluated. This paper outlines recommendations for establishing an optimized flexible power generation and electrical network architecture, illustrated by a case study of a lithium mine located at a high altitude. It was developed in partnership with our partner, Wärtsilä, and tackles sustainability from 2 angles: a solution that helps produce lithium more sustainably, and the fact that lithium is key material for EV.

Download

text:
# 计算机前沿技术在矿井/矿业领域的应用知识库

> 本知识库覆盖计算机核心前沿技术在矿业领域的典型应用场景、特殊技术难点以及推荐关注的学术期刊。供 MineIntel 科研助手进行 RAG 检索和场景分析使用。

---

## 一、计算机视觉 (Computer Vision)

### 典型应用场景
1. **井下人员检测与追踪**：基于深度学习的工人检测、行为识别（是否佩戴安全帽、是否违规操作）、人数统计、人员定位
2. **煤岩识别与智能分选**：利用机器视觉对煤炭与矸石进行自动识别和分选，替代人工手选，提高分选效率和精度
3. **皮带输送机监测**：皮带撕裂检测、异物识别（锚杆、大块煤等）、跑偏检测、堆煤/堆料检测
4. **顶板与巷道变形监测**：基于视觉的顶板离层、片帮、底鼓等变形的自动识别和预警
5. **综采工作面场景理解**：液压支架姿态识别、采煤机位置检测、刮板输送机状态监测
6. **矿区地表沉降遥感监测**：基于卫星/无人机遥感影像的地表沉陷、裂缝、滑坡等灾害识别

### 矿井特殊难点
- **光照条件极差**：井下无自然光，依赖人工照明，光源不均匀导致图像明暗差异大、阴影严重
- **煤尘/水雾遮挡**：井下煤尘浓度高、喷雾降尘产生水雾，严重干扰图像质量，导致图像退化和模糊
- **高温高湿环境**：井下温度可达 30-40°C，湿度接近 100%，镜头易起雾、凝露，设备散热困难
- **空间狭小**：巷道宽度有限（3-5m），相机安装位置受限，视野范围小，大目标无法完整拍摄
- **振动干扰**：采煤机、皮带机等大型设备运行产生强烈振动，影响图像采集稳定性
- **灾害场景极端性**：火灾、瓦斯爆炸、透水等灾害发生时，烟雾、粉尘浓度剧增，视觉系统面临极端退化
- **实时性要求高**：安全监测需要毫秒级响应，而井下计算资源有限，边缘推理面临挑战

### 推荐中文核心期刊
- 煤炭学报
- 采矿与安全工程学报
- 煤炭科学技术
- 工矿自动化
- 矿业安全与环保

## 二、自然语言处理 (Natural Language Processing)

title: 原 MineIntel 矿业计算机应用知识库
publisher: MineIntel 项目内置数据
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\data\knowledge\mining_cs_domain.md
text:
### 典型应用场景
1. **安全规程智能问答**：将《煤矿安全规程》等技术规范构建为知识图谱，支持自然语言问答
2. **事故报告自动分析**：利用 NLP 技术对煤矿事故报告进行自动分类、关键信息提取、原因分析
3. **矿山知识图谱构建**：整合矿业领域的专业知识、设备信息、地质数据等构建领域知识图谱
4. **智能调度指令理解**：理解调度员自然语言指令，自动生成分发任务或报警指令
5. **矿工培训与考核**：基于 NLP 的智能题库生成、考核评估、个性化学习路径推荐

### 矿井特殊难点
- **专业术语密集**：矿业领域有大量专业术语（如"综采工作面""锚杆支护""瓦斯突出"），通用 NLP 模型理解不足
- **文本格式不规范**：安全规程、事故报告等文档格式不统一，存在大量表格、编号混合的内容
- **中英文混合文献**：技术文献中常见英文缩写和术语混用，增加分词和理解难度
- **领域标注数据稀缺**：矿业领域的 NLP 标注数据集极少，难以训练高精度的领域模型
- **安全知识时效性**：安全规程和标准不断更新，知识库需要持续维护

### 推荐中文核心期刊
- 中国安全科学学报
- 矿业安全与环保
- 安全与环境学报

## 三、联邦学习与边缘计算 (Federated Learning & Edge Computing)

### 典型应用场景
1. **多矿井协同安全监测**：不同矿井之间通过联邦学习共享安全监测模型，不泄露各自的数据隐私
2. **井下边缘智能感知**：在井下边缘设备（如防爆计算机）上部署轻量级模型，实现本地实时推理
3. **瓦斯浓度预测**：多个矿井联合训练瓦斯浓度预测模型，提升预测精度
4. **设备故障诊断**：分布式矿井设备数据联邦训练故障诊断模型
5. **矿工健康监测**：可穿戴设备采集的生理数据通过联邦学习实现隐私保护的异常检测

### 矿井特殊难点
- **网络通信受限**：井下通信环境恶劣，WiFi/5G 信号不稳定，带宽受限，联邦学习的模型聚合面临通信瓶颈
- **数据异构性强**：不同矿井的地质条件、设备类型、开采方式差异大，数据分布高度非独立同分布（Non-IID）
- **防爆设备限制**：井下必须使用防爆型设备，算力受限，难以运行大型模型
- **实时性要求高**：安全监测场景需要低延迟响应，边缘推理需要模型轻量化
- **数据隐私合规**：矿井数据涉及安全生产和商业机密，联邦学习需要严格保证数据不出域

### 推荐中文核心期刊
- 煤炭科学技术
- 工矿自动化
- 计算机学报

text:
## 四、强化学习 (Reinforcement Learning)

### 典型应用场景
1. **矿井通风智能优化**：基于强化学习的通风系统动态调控，在保证安全的前提下降低能耗
2. **无人驾驶矿车调度**：露天矿无人驾驶矿卡的路径规划和调度优化
3. **采煤工艺参数优化**：自动调节采煤机的截割速度、牵引速度等参数以适应不同煤岩硬度
4. **矿区能源管理**：基于强化学习的矿区综合能源系统（电力、瓦斯发电、光伏）优化调度
5. **应急疏散路径规划**：灾变情况下矿工最优疏散路径的实时规划

### 矿井特殊难点
- **安全约束极其严格**：任何决策失误都可能导致人员伤亡，RL 探索过程中的安全性难以保证
- **环境动态复杂**：井下环境随开采进度不断变化，RL 的状态空间和动作空间巨大
- **仿真环境缺乏**：缺乏高保真的矿井仿真环境，RL 智能体难以在安全环境下充分训练
- **奖励函数设计困难**：安全和效率之间存在权衡，奖励函数设计需要考虑多目标优化
- **实时决策要求**：应急响应等场景需要秒级决策，RL 推理速度是关键瓶颈

### 推荐中文核心期刊
- 采矿与安全工程学报
- 系统工程理论与实践
- 控制与决策

## 五、物联网与传感器网络 (IoT & Sensor Networks)

### 典型应用场景
1. **矿井安全监测物联网**：瓦斯、温度、湿度、粉尘、一氧化碳等多参数实时监测
2. **人员定位系统**：基于 UWB/RFID/ZigBee 的井下精确定位（精度要求亚米级）
3. **设备状态监测**：采煤机、皮带机、水泵等大型设备的运行状态在线监测和故障预警
4. **矿井通信网络**：井下 5G/WiFi6/光纤通信网络的建设与优化
5. **智慧矿山综合管控平台**：整合各类传感器数据，实现矿山生产的集中监控和智能决策

### 矿井特殊难点
- **通信环境极端恶劣**：井下巷道狭长弯曲、煤壁和金属支架对无线信号衰减严重，多径效应强烈
- **防爆要求严格**：所有井下电子设备必须通过本质安全型或隔爆型认证
- **供电困难**：传感器节点依赖电池供电，在井下高温高湿环境中电池寿命缩短
- **节点数量庞大**：大型矿井需要数千个传感器节点，网络管理和数据传输压力大
- **数据异构性**：不同类型传感器（模拟量、数字量、开关量）的数据格式和采样频率差异大

### 推荐中文核心期刊
- 煤炭科学技术
- 工矿自动化
- 传感技术学报

text:
## 六、遥感与地理信息系统 (Remote Sensing & GIS)

### 典型应用场景
1. **矿区地表沉陷监测**：利用 InSAR、D-InSAR 技术监测采煤引起的地表沉降
2. **矿区生态环境监测**：基于遥感影像的土地利用/覆盖变化、植被覆盖度、水体污染监测
3. **矿区地质灾害预警**：滑坡、崩塌、泥石流等地质灾害的遥感识别和预警
4. **采空区探测**：利用高分辨率遥感 + 地面探测技术识别采空区分布
5. **矿区三维建模**：基于无人机 LiDAR/摄影测量的矿区高精度三维建模
6. **矿山复垦效果评估**：利用遥感时序数据评估矿山复垦和生态恢复效果

### 矿井特殊难点
- **云雾遮挡**：矿区多云多雨，光学遥感影像获取困难，需要 SAR 等全天候数据源
- **地形复杂**：矿区地形起伏大，遥感影像几何畸变严重，正射校正困难
- **变化检测精度**：矿区变化类型多样（塌陷、裂缝、积水等），自动变化检测精度有待提高
- **多源数据融合**：光学、SAR、LiDAR、高光谱等多源遥感数据的融合处理复杂
- **时序监测需求**：矿区变化是动态过程，需要长时间序列的遥感数据进行趋势分析

### 推荐中文核心期刊
- 遥感学报
- 测绘学报
- 中国矿业大学学报

## 七、数字孪生 (Digital Twin)

### 典型应用场景
1. **矿井数字孪生系统**：构建物理矿井的高保真虚拟镜像，实现生产过程的实时映射和预测
2. **综采工作面数字孪生**：液压支架-采煤机-刮板输送机"三机"协同的数字孪生仿真
3. **通风系统数字孪生**：矿井通风网络的实时仿真和异常预警
4. **灾害模拟与应急演练**：基于数字孪生的火灾、瓦斯爆炸等灾害模拟和应急演练平台
5. **设备全生命周期管理**：大型装备从设计、运行到报废的数字孪生健康管理

### 矿井特殊难点
- **模型复杂度极高**：矿井包含地质、机电、通风、水文等多物理场耦合，建模极其复杂
- **实时数据同步困难**：井下传感器数据到孪生系统的实时传输面临延迟和丢包问题
- **地质不确定性**：地质条件（断层、褶皱、煤层变化）存在不确定性，影响孪生精度
- **计算资源需求大**：高保真实时仿真需要大量计算资源，边缘部署困难
- **标准化程度低**：矿井数字孪生缺乏统一的数据标准和建模规范

### 推荐中文核心期刊
- 煤炭科学技术
- 计算机集成制造系统
- 系统仿真学报

## 八、大语言模型 (Large Language Model)

title: 原 MineIntel 矿业计算机应用知识库
publisher: MineIntel 项目内置数据
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\data\knowledge\mining_cs_domain.md
text:
### 典型应用场景
1. **矿山智能问答系统**：基于 LLM 的矿山安全生产知识问答，支持规程查询、技术方案推荐
2. **采矿工程设计辅助**：利用 LLM 辅助编写采矿设计方案、安全评价报告等技术文档
3. **事故分析报告生成**：基于传感器数据和事故记录，LLM 自动生成事故分析报告
4. **矿工智能培训**：基于 LLM 的交互式安全培训系统，模拟各种工况下的应对策略
5. **科研文献综述辅助**：自动检索和总结矿业领域的研究进展，辅助科研人员快速了解前沿
6. **多语言技术文献翻译**：矿业技术文献的中英互译，帮助跟踪国际研究动态

### 矿井特殊难点
- **幻觉问题严重**：安全领域对信息准确性要求极高，LLM 的幻觉可能导致严重后果
- **专业知识不足**：通用 LLM 对矿业领域知识理解有限，需要领域微调或 RAG 增强
- **数据敏感性**：矿井生产数据涉及安全机密，不能直接输入云端 LLM
- **实时性要求**：安全问答场景需要快速响应，大型 LLM 的推理延迟是挑战
- **评估标准缺失**：矿业领域缺乏 LLM 应用的标准评估基准和测试集

### 推荐中文核心期刊
- 煤炭科学技术
- 中国安全科学学报
- 计算机研究与发展

## 九、机器人与路径规划 (Robotics & Path Planning)

### 典型应用场景
1. **井下巡检机器人**：替代人工完成巷道巡检、瓦斯检测、设备状态检查等任务
2. **无人驾驶矿卡**：露天矿无人运输系统的路径规划和调度
3. **井下救援机器人**：灾后侦查、生命探测、通信中继等应急救援任务
4. **综采工作面自动化**：基于机器人的采煤机和液压支架自动控制
5. **无人机井下巡检**：密闭空间内的无人机自主飞行巡检

### 矿井特殊难点
- **GPS 信号缺失**：井下完全无 GPS 信号，机器人定位完全依赖惯性导航、激光 SLAM、视觉定位等技术
- **无障碍环境假设不成立**：巷道中存在管道、电缆、杂物等障碍物，路径规划需要实时避障
- **通信中断风险**：井下通信可能随时中断，机器人需要具备自主决策和返航能力
- **防爆要求**：机器人电机、电池等必须满足防爆标准，增加设计复杂度
- **粉尘和湿滑环境**：履带/轮式机器人在粉尘堆积和湿滑地面上的运动控制困难
- **空间约束严格**：巷道截面小、有转弯和分叉，大尺寸机器人难以通过

### 推荐中文核心期刊
- 机器人
- 采矿与安全工程学报
- 煤炭科学技术

text:
## 十、异常检测与时序分析 (Anomaly Detection & Time Series Analysis)

### 典型应用场景
1. **瓦斯涌出量异常检测**：基于时序分析的瓦斯浓度突变检测和趋势预测
2. **设备故障预警**：采煤机、皮带机、风机等关键设备的运行状态异常检测
3. **微震监测**：基于时序信号的矿震、岩爆等动力灾害前兆识别
4. **矿压监测**：液压支架工作阻力、顶板离层量等时序数据的异常分析
5. **能耗异常检测**：矿区用电、用水等能耗数据的异常识别和节能优化

### 矿井特殊难点
- **传感器噪声大**：井下恶劣环境导致传感器数据噪声高、漂移大，信号预处理困难
- **标注数据极度稀缺**：异常事件（如瓦斯突出、矿震）发生频率低，正样本极少，类别极度不平衡
- **多源异构时序**：不同传感器的采样频率和量纲不同，多源时序融合分析复杂
- **突发性异常**：瓦斯突出、突水等灾害具有突发性，检测延迟可能导致严重后果
- **环境非平稳性**：随着开采推进，传感器数据的统计特征不断变化，需要自适应检测方法

### 推荐中文核心期刊
- 采矿与安全工程学报
- 振动与冲击
- 自动化学报

title: 竞赛版矿业科研选题补充知识库
publisher: MineIntel 项目内置数据
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-research\data\knowledge\mining_research_guide.md
text:
# 矿业智能化科研知识指南

## 典型应用场景

矿井安全监测常见场景包括井下人员安全行为识别、安全帽和矿灯佩戴检测、皮带运输异物检测、矿车和人员区域入侵检测、烟雾火焰异常识别、仪表读数识别、井下巡检机器人视觉感知、瓦斯与温湿度多传感器异常预警。

适合大创或本科科研的题目应优先选择数据边界清楚、可演示、baseline 成熟的方向。例如“基于 YOLO 的煤矿皮带异物检测”“弱光粉尘条件下井下人员安全帽检测”“井下巡检机器人视觉感知与报警原型系统”。

## 井下环境技术难点

井下视觉和感知任务不同于通用室外或工厂场景。主要难点包括弱光照、局部强反光、粉尘和水雾遮挡、摄像头抖动、目标小且远、人员和设备互相遮挡、网络带宽不稳定、边缘设备算力受限、设备防爆合规要求、误报和漏报代价高。

做选题时不要只写“提升准确率”。更好的表述是围绕低照增强、小目标检测、轻量化部署、多模态融合、异常检测、误报抑制和边缘端实时性形成具体研究问题。

## 技术路线建议

计算机视觉方向可以从目标检测、语义分割、行为识别、异常检测和视觉增强切入。baseline 可选 YOLO 系列、MMDetection、Detectron2、OpenMMLab、PaddleDetection 等通用框架，再根据矿井数据做类别定义、标注格式转换、数据增强和模型轻量化。

传感器和安全预警方向可以从时间序列异常检测、瓦斯浓度预测、多源传感器融合、风险评分和报警策略优化切入。需要说明数据采集频率、缺失值处理、异常阈值和实时报警机制。

机器人方向可以从视觉感知、路径巡检、目标识别、仪表读数、远程告警和人机协同切入。大创阶段可先做仿真或小车原型，不要把完整井下无人系统作为本科项目目标。

## 评估指标

视觉检测类常用指标包括 precision、recall、mAP、F1、FPS、模型参数量、边缘设备延迟、低照/粉尘扰动下性能下降幅度。矿井安全场景尤其要关注漏报率和误报率，不能只看 mAP。

预警类任务常用指标包括 MAE、RMSE、AUC、召回率、提前预警时间、误报次数、报警延迟。报告中应解释指标与安全价值的关系。

## 数据与合规

如果没有真实矿井数据，可以采用公开数据集、公开视频片段、实验室模拟场景、合成数据或迁移学习方案。涉及学校官网、企业系统、知网、登录态平台时，不应自动登录或绕过验证码。优先使用公开网页、公开 API 和可复现实验数据。

## 报告写作要点

title: 矿井场景总览
publisher: 团队人工整理数据
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\user_sources\矿井场景总览.csv
text:
矿井场景：综采工作面
场景大类：采掘场景
痛点问题：1.采煤机截割参数凭经验设定，无法自适应煤层变化
2.液压支架自动跟机率低，人工操作劳动强度大
3.工作面直线度难以保障，易导致设备偏移
4.煤层厚度变化时采高难以自动调整
解决方案：1.智能化综采工作面：实现"记忆截割+自适应截割"联动
2.液压支架自动跟机拉移与自动纠偏
3.工作面自动找直系统
4.基于AI的采高自动调节
技术设备：1.采煤机惯性导航系统
2.液压支架电液控制系统
3.工作面视频监控+AI识别
4.刮板输送机工况监测传感器
5.LASC直线度检测系统
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；《2024智能矿山暨无人驾驶行业蓝皮书》；国家能源局《煤矿智能化标准体系建设指南》(2024)

矿井场景：掘进工作面
场景大类：采掘场景
痛点问题：1.掘进机截割精度依赖人工经验，超欠挖严重
2.临时支护与永久支护衔接不畅，效率低
3.掘进工作面粉尘大，能见度低
4.地质条件未知，前方探水探瓦斯困难
5.掘进与运输、通风协调不足
解决方案：1.智能掘进成套装备：截割+支护+运输+除尘一体化
2.掘进机自主定位定向截割
3.远程遥控/自动化掘进
4.超前地质探测与智能导引
5.掘支平行作业系统
技术设备：1.掘进机机身位姿感知系统（惯性导航+全站仪）
2.截割轨迹规划与自动控制软件
3.机载临时支护机器人
4.综合除尘成套设备
5.超前地质探测仪（地质雷达/瞬变电磁仪）
6.工业摄像头+边缘计算盒子
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；华为《矿山智能化白皮书》；《金属矿山数字化转型白皮书》(华为)

矿井场景：穿爆作业（露天矿）
场景大类：采掘场景
痛点问题：1.爆破参数设计凭经验，块度不均匀
2.爆破振动对周边建筑/边坡影响不可控
3.炮孔利用率低，大块率高
4.爆破安全距离内人员管理困难
解决方案：1.智能穿爆系统：基于地质模型的爆破参数优化
2.爆破振动实时监测与预警
3.数字化爆破设计与管理平台
4.爆破区域智能安全管控
技术设备：1.智能钻机（自动布孔+精确定位）
2.爆破设计软件
3.爆破振动监测仪
4.高精度GPS/RTK定位系统
5.无人机航拍+三维建模系统
参考来源：《2024智能矿山暨无人驾驶行业蓝皮书》；《中国智能矿山产业发展白皮书》(亿欧智库)

title: 矿井场景总览

text:
矿井场景：井下主运输系统（带式输送机）
场景大类：运输场景
痛点问题：1.输送带跑偏、撕裂、断带等故障频发
2.多台皮带机联动控制效率低，空载率高
3.输送机沿线巡检依赖人工，劳动强度大
4.大倾角/弯曲运输工况适应性差
5.煤流分布不均导致运力浪费
解决方案：1.输送机智能运维：实时故障预测与健康管理(PHM)
2.多机智能联动与煤流均衡调度
3.机器人沿线智能巡检替代人工
4.输送带防跑偏/防撕裂智能保护
5.煤量智能识别与变频调速
技术设备：1.输送带防跑偏/防撕裂传感器
2.矿用巡检机器人（轨道式/履带式）
3.变频驱动器(VFD)
4.煤量检测雷达/称重传感器
5.PLC控制系统+SCADA平台
6.红外测温/振动传感器
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；《2024智能矿山暨无人驾驶行业蓝皮书》；国家能源局《煤矿智能化标准体系建设指南》(2024)

矿井场景：井下辅助运输
场景大类：运输场景
痛点问题：1.无轨胶轮车/电机车调度依赖人工，效率低
2.井下道路狭窄、交叉点多，运输安全隐患大
3.车辆超速、违规驾驶难以实时管控
4.物料/人员运输缺乏统一调度
5.新能源车辆充电管理困难
解决方案：1.井下辅助运输智能调度系统
2.无人驾驶/远程驾驶辅助运输
3.车辆实时定位与轨迹跟踪
4.统一的"人-车-物料"调度平台
5.新能源车辆智能充电管理
技术设备：1.5G专网+V2X车路协同设备
2.井下高精度定位系统（UWB/BLE）
3.车载终端+自动驾驶控制器
4.智能交通信号灯/道岔控制器
5.激光雷达+毫米波雷达+摄像头
6.充电桩智能管理系统
参考来源：《2024智能矿山暨无人驾驶行业蓝皮书》；中国电信《5G智慧矿山融合方案白皮书》；华为《矿山智能化白皮书》

矿井场景：露天矿卡车运输
场景大类：运输场景
痛点问题：1.大型矿卡人力成本高，司机招聘难
2.疲劳驾驶导致安全事故
3.运输路线固定但调度效率低
4.车辆利用率不高，空驶率高
5.燃油消耗大，碳排放高
解决方案：1.露天矿无人驾驶运输系统
2.车队智能调度与路径优化
3.纯电动/新能源矿卡替代
4.装卸载全流程自动化
5.运输数据分析与运营优化
技术设备：1.无人驾驶矿卡
2.高精度卫星定位（RTK-GNSS）
3.车载计算平台+自动驾驶算法
4.智能调度平台
5.电铲/挖掘机协同装载系统
6.V2X通信设备
参考来源：《2024智能矿山暨无人驾驶行业蓝皮书》；《2025智能矿山暨无人驾驶行业蓝皮书》(亿欧智库)；《中央企业AI应用场景白皮书》(2024)

title: 矿井场景总览
publisher: 团队人工整理数据

text:
矿井场景：瓦斯监测监控
场景大类：安全监控
痛点问题：1.瓦斯传感器精度不足，误报率高
2.传感器维护标定频繁，工作量大
3.瓦斯超限后应急响应不及时
4.瓦斯抽采系统与监测联动不足
5.大面积瓦斯监测覆盖不完整
解决方案：1.瓦斯智能监测预警系统
2.基于AI的瓦斯涌出量预测
3.瓦斯超限智能联动控制（自动断电/通风）
4.瓦斯抽采系统智能调控
5.传感器远程标定与自诊断
技术设备：1.红外/激光瓦斯传感器（新一代）
2.多参数环境监测传感器（瓦斯+CO+温度+风速）
3.瓦斯抽采在线监控装置
4.边缘计算网关
5.安全监控综合平台软件
6.智能断电控制器
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；国家能源局《煤矿智能化标准体系建设指南》(2024)；《2026年AI+智慧矿山全场景应用解决方案白皮书》

矿井场景：水害防治
场景大类：安全监控
痛点问题：1.井下突水/透水事故预警手段不足
2.老空区/含水层水文参数监测不全面
3.排水系统自动化程度低
4.应急预案执行依赖人工决策
5.地表水体与井下联系分析不充分
解决方案：1.矿井水害智能监测预警系统
2.水文动态多源信息融合分析
3.排水系统自动化与智能调控
4.透水事故智能应急联动
5.地表-井下立体化水情监测
技术设备：1.水文监测传感器（水位/水压/流量/水温）
2.瞬变电磁探测仪（超前探水）
3.智能排水泵站及自动控制系统
4.微震监测系统
5.地质雷达
6.水情预警平台软件
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；《2026年AI+智慧矿山全场景应用解决方案白皮书》；国家能源局《煤矿智能化标准体系建设指南》(2024)

矿井场景：火灾与防灭火
场景大类：安全监控
痛点问题：1.煤层自燃发火早期征兆难以识别
2.采空区温度/CO监测覆盖不全
3.防灭火材料注入效果无法评估
4.外因火灾（电缆/设备起火）发现滞后
5.火灾发生后风流调控不及时
解决方案：1.煤自然发火智能预测预警
2.采空区多参数（温度+CO+O2）立体监测
3.智能注氮/注浆防灭火系统
4.电缆/设备火灾热成像监控
5.火灾智能通风联动控制
技术设备：1.分布式光纤温度传感系统(DTS)
2.束管监测系统
3.红外热成像仪
4.智能注氮站/注浆站
5.气体分析色谱仪
6.矿用阻燃电缆火灾探测器
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；《2026年AI+智慧矿山全场景应用解决方案白皮书》

title: 矿井场景总览
publisher: 团队人工整理数据
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\user_sources\矿井场景总览.csv
text:
矿井场景：顶板与矿压监测
场景大类：安全监控
痛点问题：1.顶板来压规律预测精度低
2.支架工作阻力监测不连续
3.围岩变形监测手段单一
4.冲击地压预警手段不足
5.大采高/放顶煤工作面矿压显现复杂
解决方案：1.矿压在线智能监测与预警
2.支架工作阻力连续监测与来压预测
3.围岩变形多源信息融合分析
4.冲击地压多参量综合预警
5.基于AI的顶板管理决策支持
技术设备：1.矿压监测传感器（压力/位移/应力）
2.微震监测系统
3.钻孔应力计
4.顶板离层仪
5.支架压力监测分站
6.矿压数据分析预警平台
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；国家能源局《煤矿智能化标准体系建设指南》(2024)；《2026年AI+智慧矿山全场景应用解决方案白皮书》

矿井场景：矿井通风系统
场景大类：通风场景
痛点问题：1.通风网络复杂，风量分配不合理
2.通风能耗高，主扇运行效率低
3.局部通风机管理分散
4.通风设施（风门/风桥）状态监测不足
5.灾变时期风流调控手段有限
解决方案：1.智能通风系统：按需通风与风量优化
2.主通风机变频节能运行
3.局部通风机智能联动控制
4.通风设施状态在线监测
5.灾变通风智能应急调控
技术设备：1.风速/风压/风量传感器
2.主通风机变频器+在线监测
3.智能风门/风窗执行机构
4.通风网络解算软件
5.气体检测传感器
6.环境监测分站
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；《中国智能矿山产业发展白皮书》(亿欧智库)；国家能源局《煤矿智能化标准体系建设指南》(2024)

矿井场景：矿井通信网络
场景大类：通信与信息化
痛点问题：1.井下无线通信信号覆盖不完整
2.多种通信系统并存（WiFi/4G/5G/广播），互不兼容
3.语音/数据/视频传输带宽不足
4.通信设备防爆认证要求高
5.地面与井下通信可靠性差
解决方案：1.建设5G+WiFi6融合的矿井通信网络
2.统一的矿山通信平台（语音+数据+视频）
3.井下核心网下沉（MEC边缘计算）
4.应急通信保障体系
5.矿鸿(MineHarmony)统一物联协议
技术设备：1.矿用5G基站（宏站+微站+基站）
2.矿用防爆手机/终端
3.MEC边缘计算平台
4.工业环网交换机
5.融合通信调度平台
6.矿鸿协议智能终端
参考来源：中国电信《5G智慧矿山融合方案白皮书》；华为《矿山智能化白皮书》；中国电信《5G智慧矿山终端融合白皮书》

title: 矿井场景总览
publisher: 团队人工整理数据
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\user_sources\矿井场景总览.csv
text:
矿井场景：井下人员定位与管理
场景大类：人员管理
痛点问题：1.传统定位精度低（区域级），无法精确到人
2.人员入井/升井管理依赖人工
3.限员管理困难，超员风险高
4.灾变时人员搜救定位不准
5.特殊岗位人员在岗状态不明
解决方案：1.高精度井下人员定位系统（亚米级/厘米级）
2.智能入井考勤与限员管理
3.人员轨迹分析与行为识别
4.应急搜救精确定位
5.电子围栏与禁区管控
技术设备：1.UWB/BLE定位基站与标签
2.人脸识别闸机
3.定位管理平台软件
4.防爆智能安全帽（集成定位+通信）
5.人员行为AI分析系统
6.电子围栏系统
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；《中国智能矿山产业发展白皮书》(亿欧智库)；国家能源局《煤矿智能化标准体系建设指南》(2024)

矿井场景：大型固定设备运维（主提升/主排水/压风等）
场景大类：设备运维
痛点问题：1.设备检修依赖定期计划，过度维修或维修不足
2.设备故障停机影响生产，损失大
3.关键设备运行参数监测不全面
4.设备健康状态评估缺乏数据支撑
5.备品备件管理粗放
解决方案：1.设备预测性维护（PHM）系统
2.关键设备在线状态监测
3.基于AI的设备故障诊断与寿命预测
4.设备全生命周期管理平台
5.智能备件管理与库存优化
技术设备：1.振动传感器/温度传感器/油液分析传感器
2.PLC数据采集网关
3.设备健康管理平台(PHM)
4.工业大数据分析平台
5.智能巡检仪/手持终端
6.数字孪生仿真系统
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；ABB《Next Level Mining》白皮书；《2024智能矿山暨无人驾驶行业蓝皮书》

矿井场景：地质保障与三维建模
场景大类：地质保障
痛点问题：1.地质构造探查精度不够，前方隐伏构造难发现
2.地质模型更新滞后，与实际偏差大
3.多源地质数据融合分析能力弱
4.工作面前方煤层厚度/岩性变化预判不足
5.掘进/回采中地质异常影响生产
解决方案：1.透明地质/透明工作面系统
2.三维地质模型动态更新
3.多源地质数据融合分析平台
4.基于AI的地质异常智能识别
5.随掘/随采地震超前探测
技术设备：1.三维地震勘探设备
2.槽波地震仪
3.瞬变电磁仪/地质雷达
4.三维地质建模软件
5.钻孔测井仪
6.地质大数据分析平台
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；《金属矿山数字化转型白皮书》(华为)；《2026年AI+智慧矿山全场景应用解决方案白皮书》

title: 矿井场景总览
publisher: 团队人工整理数据
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\user_sources\矿井场景总览.csv
text:
矿井场景：选矿/洗煤生产
场景大类：选矿加工
痛点问题：1.选矿/洗煤工艺参数人工调节，产品质量波动
2.核心分选设备（重介旋流器/浮选机）智能化程度低
3.尾矿品位偏高，回收率不稳定
4.选矿过程能耗高、药剂消耗大
5.在线检测滞后，无法实时反馈
解决方案：1.智能选矿/洗煤全流程控制
2.核心分选设备智能控制（重介密度/浮选液位）
3.基于AI的工艺参数优化与质量预测
4.在线品位分析与实时反馈控制
5.尾矿综合利用智能管理
技术设备：1.在线X荧光分析仪(XRF)/在线灰分仪
2.核心分选设备智能控制器
3.工业摄像头+AI矿石品位识别
4.变频器+智能阀门执行器
5.浮选/重介工艺优化软件
6.选矿DCS控制系统
参考来源：《中国智能矿山产业发展白皮书》(亿欧智库)；《金属矿山数字化转型白皮书》(华为)；《2024智能矿山暨无人驾驶行业蓝皮书》

矿井场景：矿井供电系统
场景大类：供电保障
痛点问题：1.井下供电可靠性要求高但故障频发
2.供电系统单线图与实际拓扑不同步
3.继电保护整定复杂，协调困难
4.电能质量监测不完善
5.谐波治理与无功补偿不足
解决方案：1.智能供电管理系统
2.供电网络拓扑自动识别
3.智能继电保护与故障自愈
4.电能质量在线监测与治理
5.变配电房智能巡检
技术设备：1.智能综合保护装置
2.电能质量分析仪
3.配电房巡检机器人
4.智能开关柜
5.供电监控SCADA系统
6.无功补偿与谐波治理装置
参考来源：《煤矿智能化发展蓝皮书》2025；国家能源局《煤矿智能化标准体系建设指南》(2024)

矿井场景：矿山综合管控平台
场景大类：综合管理
痛点问题：1.各子系统（通风/排水/运输/安全/供电等）信息孤岛严重
2.调度指挥依赖电话+对讲机，效率低
3.生产数据分散，无法形成统一决策依据
4.缺乏全局性的生产态势感知
5.应急指挥缺乏多系统联动支撑
解决方案：1.矿山综合管控平台（一体化平台）
2.生产调度智能指挥中心
3.多系统数据融合与统一展示
4.基于数字孪生的矿山态势感知
5.应急指挥多系统联动
技术设备：1.综合管控平台软件
2.大屏可视化系统
3.数据中台/数据湖
4.数字孪生引擎
5.统一指挥调度台
6.AI决策支持系统
参考来源：华为《矿山智能化白皮书》；《中国智能矿山产业发展白皮书》(亿欧智库)；《2026年AI+智慧矿山全场景应用解决方案白皮书》

title: 矿井场景总览
publisher: 团队人工整理数据
local_file: D:\chuangye\IntelliSearch\autoclaw_submit\mineintel-application-kg\data\user_sources\矿井场景总览.csv
text:
矿井场景：AI大模型赋能
场景大类：综合管理
痛点问题：1.矿山专业AI算法开发门槛高、周期长
2.各场景AI模型重复开发、通用性差
3.AI工业机理模型与矿山专业知识结合不足
4.缺乏统一的AI开发与部署平台
5.数据标注与训练样本积累不足
解决方案：1.矿山AI大模型一体化平台
2.基于大模型的低代码AI开发
3.矿山专业领域AI模型库
4.AI模型在线推理与持续迭代
5.矿山知识图谱与智能问答
技术设备：1.AI一体机（推理/训练服务器）
2.矿山AI大模型（如国家能源集团AI平台/中煤"地知"大模型）
3.GPU/NPU算力集群
4.MLOps平台
5.数据标注工具
6.矿山知识图谱构建工具
参考来源：《2026年AI+智慧矿山全场景应用解决方案白皮书》；华为《矿山智能化白皮书》；《中央企业AI应用场景白皮书》(2024)

矿井场景：绿色低碳与环保
场景大类：综合管理
痛点问题：1.矿山碳排放核算不准确
2.矸石山/尾矿库环境监测不完善
3.矿井水处理达标率不稳定
4.粉尘/噪声污染治理手段落后
5.绿色矿山评价缺乏数据支撑
解决方案：1.矿山碳排放智能监测与管理
2.矸石山/尾矿库在线安全监测
3.矿井水处理智能控制系统
4.粉尘/噪声在线监测与自动降尘
5.绿色矿山评价指标数据化管理
技术设备：1.碳排放监测传感器
2.环境监测站（粉尘/噪声/水质）
3.智能喷雾降尘系统
4.矿井水处理PLC控制系统
5.环保数据管理平台
6.无人机环保巡检系统
参考来源：《中国智能矿山产业发展白皮书》(亿欧智库)；《2024智能矿山暨无人驾驶行业蓝皮书》；FARO《Mapping the Underground World》白皮书
